title: Package Management & Software Installation
summary: Learn how software is managed on Linux systems. Understand package managers (apt, yum/dnf), repositories, dependencies, and how to compile from source. This knowledge is essential for provisioning and maintaining servers.

resources.title: Linux Package Management - apt, yum, dnf Explained

questions.questionText: Which command updates the package list on Ubuntu/Debian?
questions.explanation: 'apt update' refreshes the local package index from the configured repositories. This does not upgrade any packages — it only downloads the latest list of available packages.

questions.questionText: What is the equivalent of 'apt' on CentOS/RHEL/Fedora?
questions.explanation: CentOS/RHEL uses 'yum' (older) or 'dnf' (newer, default on Fedora and RHEL 8+). Both manage RPM packages, while apt manages DEB packages.

A repository is a server (or collection of servers) that hosts software packages. Package managers download packages from these repositories. You can add third-party repositories for additional software.

options.text: A Git repository for source code
A server that hosts installable software packages
A local directory with configuration files

tasks.title: Package Manager Essentials
tasks.description:
1. Update the package list: `sudo apt update` (Debian/Ubuntu) or `sudo dnf check-update` (Fedora/RHEL)
2. Search for a package: `apt search htop` or `dnf search htop`
3. Show package info: `apt show htop` or `dnf info htop`
4. Install a package: `sudo apt install htop` or `sudo dnf install htop`
5. List installed packages: `dpkg -l | head -20` or `rpm -qa | head -20`
6. Check which package a file belongs to: `dpkg -S /usr/bin/git` or `rpm -qf /usr/bin/git`
7. Remove a package: `sudo apt remove htop` or `sudo dnf remove htop`
8. Clean up cached packages: `sudo apt autoremove && sudo apt clean`
hints: Always run 'apt update' before 'apt install' to ensure you get the latest version
Use 'apt list --installed' to see all installed packages on Debian/Ubuntu

tasks.title: Install Software from Source
tasks.description:
1. Install build essentials: `sudo apt install build-essential` or `sudo dnf groupinstall 'Development Tools'`
2. Clone a small project from GitHub: `git clone https://github.com/jqlang/jq.git` (or download a release tarball)
3. Alternatively, download a release: `wget https://github.com/jqlang/jq/releases/download/jq-1.7.1/jq-1.7.1.tar.gz`
4. Extract: `tar -xzf jq-1.7.1.tar.gz && cd jq-1.7.1`
5. Read the README/INSTALL file
6. Build: `autoreconf -i && ./configure && make`
7. Install: `sudo make install`
8. Verify: `jq --version`
9. Reflect: Why might you install from source vs. using a package manager?
hints: The classic build pattern is: ./configure && make && sudo make install
If autoreconf is missing, install it with: sudo apt install autoconf automake libtool
